policy.storage: Link-only review queue. Do not download, scrape, vendor, train on, or package third-party photos, footage, captions, comments, thumbnails, or audio.
policy.promotion_gate: Every promoted item needs creator, URL, date, approximate station or reach, observed flow or weather context, license or written permission, attribution, and allowed-use notes.
policy.allowed_use_before_rights_clear: Manual visual comparison, guide discussion, source-planning notes, and internal annotation placeholders only.
policy.blocked_use_before_rights_clear: Texture extraction, photogrammetry, AI training, marketing screenshots, packaged media, copied captions, or derived shipping assets.

review_targets.anchor_hint: Chili Bar to Coloma guide-seat downstream frame
visual_questions: Are exposed rocks, bank trees, and water color believable at summer commercial flow?
Can a swimmer and throw-rope recovery zone remain visible behind foam and boulders?

review_targets.anchor_hint: Low-flow rock exposure and wet-bank comparison
visual_questions: Which boulder faces should be dry, damp, or freshly splashed?
How much yellow grass and oak scrub should appear on late-season banks?

review_targets.anchor_hint: Spring runoff or release-driven high-flow review
visual_questions: Where should foam streaks and eddy fences strengthen without hiding hazards?
How high should wet banks and splash marks climb at high runnable flow?

review_targets.anchor_hint: Lees Ferry / Grand Canyon river-eye downstream frame
visual_questions: Do canyon walls read at Grand Canyon scale from low raft height?
Are sandbars, talus, and sparse riparian shrubs distributed believably?
Which sandbars should be exposed or damp across low, moderate, and high release bands?
Do current seams, boils, and lateral waves stay readable for an oarsman?

review_targets.anchor_hint: Long-water rescue and oar-rig sightline review
visual_questions: Can the player read swimmer position at Grand Canyon scale?
Do boils, wave trains, canyon shadows, and oar frame foreground avoid hiding rescue targets?

review_targets.anchor_hint: Lower Pacuare rainforest gorge guide-seat frame
visual_questions: How dense should canopy, vines, wet rock, and shadow be before rescue targets become unreadable?
What water color and humidity/mist cues distinguish clear-season from rain-fed conditions?

review_targets.anchor_hint: Waterfall, mist, and wet-rock detail pass
visual_questions: Where should mist and side waterfalls add atmosphere without masking line choice?
Which wet-rock classes need procedural material variants before first-party field capture exists?
Can a swimmer be tracked under tree-shadow and mist?
Which foreground leaves and branches must be culled or faded near rescue targets?

next_actions: Open each candidate source manually and record only links plus observed conditions until rights are explicit.
Attach guide or outfitter notes to each promoted reference target before it changes art, water, or gameplay tuning.
Prefer first-party capture for any texture, photogrammetry, marketing, or AI-training use.
